articles.title: Vedanta demerger: Did the Anil Agarwal-led stock really crash 59% today? Here's what happened
articles.summary: Vedanta's share price fell 59% to ₹310 on April 30 due to a demerger adjustment. Despite this, the company reported record profits, with Q4FY26 PAT at ₹9,352 crore, up 89% YoY. The demerger will create five independently listed entities.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Rupee depreciates 0.2% to open at 95.02 against US dollar amid surge in crude oil prices, hawkish US Fed
articles.summary: Rupee depreciates 0.2% to open at 95.02 against US dollar amid surge in crude oil prices, hawkish US Fed
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Silver price today: White metal rebounds to ₹2.35 lakh/kg as dollar falls after Federal Reserve rate decision
articles.summary: Silver prices in India recovered on Thursday after reaching a one-month low, supported by a weaker U.S. dollar. However, high crude oil prices raise inflation concerns, impacting interest rates. MCX silver rose 1% to ₹2,35,102 per kg, while gold prices remained stable.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold rate up on MCX after US Fed hold rates steady; a sharp jump in crude oil prices raises inflation fears
articles.summary: Gold rate climbed on MCX on Thursday morning after the US Fed held rates steady. The US Federal Reserve on 29 April kept the federal funds rate unchanged for the third consecutive policy at 3.5%–3.75%.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: HFCL is attracting strong investor interest. Where is the stock headed?
articles.summary: The stock of HFCL has surged more than 50% in a month. The question now is, will the rally sustain?
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.summary: The Indian stock indices Nifty 50 and Sensex saw gains on April 29, boosted by stock-specific advancements. Analysts suggest buying Vedanta, IndusInd Bank, Tata Consumer, Siemens Energy, and Navin Fluorine as the market faces pressures from rising oil prices and geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.title: Vedanta Demerger LIVE Updates: Special price discovery session begins; stock to trade ex-demerger price

articles.title: Crude nears $120 as Trump signals Hormuz blockade to continue
articles.summary: Brent crude traded close to $120 a barrel, following comments by US President Donald Trump that Washington would not end the ongoing naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz until a nuclear deal is signed with Iran.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: AI-Led Rally in Asia Stocks Masks Deeper Damage from Iran War
articles.summary: Asia’s artificial intelligence-fueled rally is masking signs of strain, with gains in tech names overshadowing the impact of the US-Iran war on the broader market.

articles.title: US Fed holds rates at 3.5%–3.75% for third straight policy: What does it mean for Indian stock market?
articles.summary: The FOMC voted 8-4 to hold the benchmark interest rate in a range steady and highlighted that inflation is elevated. The April policy meeting concluded on predicted lines, without any economic projections.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Jerome Powell plans to remain on US Fed's board after term as chariman ends next month
articles.summary: After my term as chair ends on May 15, I will continue to serve as a governor for a period of time to be determined, Jerome Powell said at the press conference that was broadcast at midnight on Thursday in India.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US Federal Reserve keeps rates unchanged for third straight meeting: 5 key takeaways from April policy
articles.summary: The US Federal Reserve on 29 April kept the federal funds rate unchanged for the third consecutive meeting at 3.5%–3.75% amdi increased risk of inflation rising due to higher global energy prices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vedanta demerger: When will four spun-off entities of the mining major trade on Dalal Street? CEO answers
articles.summary: According to a PTI report, Vedanta Resources CEO Deshnee Naidoo said that its India arm will file with stock exchanges next week for listing approval of its demerged entities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 earnings preview: Brokerages see steady NII growth, muted profit expansion
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank will announce its Q4FY26 results on 2 May. Brokerages expect steady growth in net interest income and operating profitability, but subdued net profit due to margin pressure. The stock has gained 8% in April, yet remains down 13% year to date.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Bloom Energy stock hits fresh record, jumps 28% after blockbuster Q1 results and higher 2026 guidance
articles.summary: Bloom Energy's shares soared 28.32% to $290.50 after a strong Q1 FY26 report, with revenues up 130.4% to $751.1 million. The company raised its full-year revenue outlook to $3.4-$3.8 billion, driven by demand from data centres and partnerships with Oracle.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices surge over 7%, extend gains to 8th day; Brent tops $119 — What's behind this sharp rally?
articles.summary: US crude rose 7.6% to $107.52 a barrel, and Brent rose to $119,54 per barrel, up 7.44% after the EIA, after the US oil stocks plummeted sharply and amid little signs of resolution to the Middle East crisis that's entered its third month now.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Trump's US Fed chief nominee Kevin Warsh clears key confirmation hurdle in Senate
articles.summary: The Senate Banking Committee voted along party lines to advance Warsh's nomination to the full Republican-controlled Senate. All 13 Republicans on the panel supported Warsh.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Ahead of IPO, founders invest ₹40 crore in digital lending platform Kissht at premium
articles.summary: Founders Ranvir Singh and Krishnan Vishwanathan have collectively invested at ₹201 per share, higher than the IPO price band of ₹162-171, signalling confidence in the business, they added.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Google, Alphabet to Meta: 4 tech mega-caps among ~270 US companies set to post March quarter results today
articles.summary: Among the mega-caps slated to report January-March quarter results are Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta Platforms. Overall, 269 companies will post their quarterly earnings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold slides $86/oz, silver drops $1.5/oz ahead of US Fed decision, oil surge
articles.summary: Precious metals continued to decline, with gold futures dropping $86 to $4,522 and silver futures falling $1.5 to $71.71. Rising crude oil prices heighten inflation concerns, prompting expectations of sustained higher interest rates from central banks and impacting the appeal of these metals.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Jerome Powell is staying at the Fed. That isn’t Kevin Warsh’s biggest problem.Story
articles.summary: Warsh, Powell’s presumed successor as Federal Reserve chair, will take the reins in mid-May with Fed officials divided in their policy outlook and his predecessor still at the table.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: ‘Can’t get a job anywhere else’: Trump blasts Powell staying on Fed board
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump criticized Fed chief Jerome Powell, claiming he is staying at the Fed due to lack of job options. Powell confirmed he will remain on the Board until a criminal investigation concludes, while the Fed's decision saw dissent from four officials for the first time since 1992.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt mulls partial credit guarantee, interest subvention support for pvt sector electric bus, truck adoption
articles.summary: The heavy industries ministry is consulting stakeholders on partial credit guarantees and interest subsidies to make electric buses and trucks affordable for private operators, who run 90% of the country's buses but have been excluded from existing federal electric vehicle incentive schemes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt working on resuming Shipping Corp of India's maritime services to West Asia
articles.summary: External affairs minister S. Jaishankar spoke to his Iranian counterpart Seyed Abbas Araghchi regarding the current situation in West Asia, amid reports US President Donad Trump had rejected Iran's proposal to end the war and remove Hormuz blockade.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India aims for $2 tn total exports by FY31 with an MSME, agri push
articles.summary: The $2 trillion export target includes $1 trillion each in merchandise shipments and $1 trillion in services, the commerce ministry said.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: West Asia war, weak monsoon pose downside risk to growth, says finance ministry
articles.summary: The review also noted that passing on higher energy costs to the final consumer may be inevitable. Some countries have begun to allow prices to be passed on to end-users - households and businesses - but some are yet to do so, it said.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Summer crops resilient, but prolonged heat may dent output, says ICAR chief
articles.summary: Field reports suggest that these short-duration crops, grown between rabi and kharif seasons, are largely holding up, supported by timely irrigation, said M.L. Jat, secretary, Department of Agricultural Research and Education, and director general, Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR).
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: China stays on top, India slips 3 spots: How the West Asia war changed EM ranks
articles.summary: With crude above $100 and capital flows turning volatile, the West Asia shock is now playing out through financial channels. Emerging market rankings are shifting, driven by external resilience rather than growth.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Will Iran war shake India's flagship welfare schemes? The finance ministry wants to find out
articles.summary: Schemes under review include the Pradhan Mantri Vishwakarma Yojana; Mudra Yojana; Svanidhi Yojana; and Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana. The review will check whether external shocks could affect the implementation of these schemes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt mulls customs duty relief on cotton import to support textile industry
articles.summary: Raw cotton imports into India attract a 5% basic customs duty. The government had exempted the commodity from import duty from 19 August to 30 September 2025. This was later extended till 31 December 2025.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's industrial output growth eases to five-month low of 4.1% in March
articles.summary: The data follows the reading in core sector which showed a contraction by 0.4% in March, marking its lowest level in 19 months, compared with a revised 2.8% growth in February and 4.5% expansion in March 2025
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's fertilizer subsidy may jump 20% as Hormuz crisis spikes prices
articles.summary: India's fertilizer subsidy stood at ₹1.86 trillion in the 2025-26 fiscal year (FY26), and was estimated at ₹1.71 trillion in the budget for the ongoing financial year. Mint reported on 2 March that India's fertilizer subsidy will go up amid the West Asian conflict.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India seals FTA with New Zealand, gets $20 bn investment pledge, duty-free access
articles.summary: The agreement comes at a time of evolving global and regional dynamics, with New Zealand viewing India as a key partner in promoting regional stability, economic resilience and shared prosperity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India-New Zealand FTA: Temporary Employment Entry offers 5,000 work visas for skilled Indians — check key features
articles.summary: The TEE pathway caps the number of visas at 5,000 at any time, allowing stays of up to 3 years. It targets both traditional Indian expertise—such as yoga instructors, AYUSH practitioners, and chefs—and high-demand fields like IT, healthcare, engineering, and education.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mapping the fallout: Which Indian sectors face risks from the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war has triggered an energy crisis, hitting sectors like fertilizers and airlines. With the Strait of Hormuz disrupted, rising costs and supply shortages could directly or indirectly impact nearly 30 sectors.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil jumps over 1% as US-Iran talks stall, Hormuz blockade deepens crisis
articles.summary: Crude prices rose over 1% after US-Iran talks stalled and Hormuz tensions deepened, raising fears of supply disruptions and sharper inventory draws.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India to overhaul firefighting infra, rejig its vehicles and equipment
articles.summary: The government is seeking to address critical gaps in fire safety infrastructure, amid acute shortages of fire stations, equipment and personnel, raising concerns over response readiness as urban fire risks rise.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The FTA cautionary tale: What India-Korea trade warns us about new global deals
articles.summary: In their bilateral trade pact, South Korea has gained significantly more than India. As it enters more such pacts, India would do well to review where it fell short with South Korea and why.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Kedaara picks up 5% in Axis Finance for Rs750 crore, sets benchmark for IPO-headed NBFC
articles.summary: Axis Finance has raised Rs750 crore from Kedaara Capital for a 5.08% stake, marking its first external funding. This capital boost strengthens its financial base as it jostles in India's extremely competitive credit market—preparing for a future IPO under regulatory requirements.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Fertilizer output, IT earnings, health spending survey
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre raises wheat procurement target to 34.5 million tonnes amid erratic weather
articles.summary: The government has raised procurement target even as unseasonal rains raise output concerns.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Oil prices up 1% amid continued stalemate on US-Iran talks, closure of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Crude prices climbed over 1% as supply disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and stalled US-Iran talks kept markets on edge, raising concerns over India’s import bill and fuel marketing losses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI sees growth, inflation risks from escalating US-Iran war
articles.summary: Further escalation, a longer duration and a wider spread of the war are key risks to the global outlook, says RBI in its April Bulletin
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Power demand spikes even before peak summer, seen at record high this year
articles.summary: India’s power demand has surged to nearly 240 GW amid an early, intense heatwave, nearing last year’s peak. With temperatures set to stay above normal, demand could hit record highs, testing grid capacity despite rising supply and preparedness by power utilities.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Indian private sector output growth accelerates in April as manufacturing grows faster: HSBC Flash India PMI
articles.summary: April saw a rebound in India's private sector growth. The HSBC Flash PMI revealed stronger manufacturing output and job creation, although inflation pressures remained high, with firms raising selling prices in response.
articles.label: BULLISH
